mnemonics.title: Wait Macros Memory Aid
mnemonics.content:
Z-E-T: Zombies Exist Till wait() called
- Z - Zombies created when parent ignores
- E - Exit status obtained by WEXITSTATUS
- T - Termination checked by WIFEXITED (true=normal) or WIFSIGNALED (true=signal)

mnemonics.title: wait vs waitpid
mnemonics.content:
WAIT: Wait for Any Child Termination
waitpid: Wait with PID control + Options (WNOHANG, WUNTRACED)

mnemonics.title: Process States
mnemonics.content:
ORPHAN: Original Parent Had No Return - child becomes orphan when parent dies
ZOMBIE: Zero Output Means Born Zombie Entry - terminated child waiting for parent wait()

acronyms.usage: Checks if child terminated normally via exit() or return from main()
Extracts exit code from status after WIFEXITED returns true
Checks if child was terminated by an uncaught signal
Option for waitpid() to return immediately without blocking
Returns the signal number that caused child termination
Option to report stopped child processes

visualTips.visualization: Draw process tree: Parent → fork() → Child runs → Child exits → Child box marked 'ZOMBIE' → Parent calls wait() → Child removed
Flowchart: Parent calls wait() → Decision diamond: Any child terminated? → No: Block parent → Yes: Return child PID + status → Process child exit code
Timeline: Parent creates Child → Parent dies → Child becomes ORPHAN → Arrow from Child to init(process 1) → init adopts Child → init calls wait() → Child reaped

analogies.analogy: Like a delivery tracking - package (child) delivered but not picked up (wait called), it sits in holding area (process table) until picked up.
Like a child whose parents died - becomes ward of state (init process) which takes responsibility.
Like checking mailbox without waiting - you check if there's mail (child exited) and return immediately if empty, rather than waiting by the box.
